name: pyhf (Pure Python HistFactory Statistical Models)

performance.notes: JAX/PyTorch/TensorFlow backends for GPU-accelerated fits; NumPy default

agent_readiness.notes: HistFactory statistical models for HEP. import pyhf. model = pyhf.simplemodels.uncorrelated_background(signal=[12.0], bkg=[50.0], bkg_uncertainty=[7.0]). data = pyhf.tensorlib.astensor([51.0] + model.config.auxdata). CLs_obs, CLs_exp = pyhf.infer.hypotest(1.0, data, model, test_stat='qtilde'). pyhf.set_backend('jax'). Read HistFactory JSON: model, data = pyhf.utils.load_jsonpatch_set(). Asymptotic approximation. Toy-based CLs. ATLAS HEPData JSON. pyhf.infer.intervals.

gotchas: Highly domain-specific — HEP HistFactory statistics only
Requires understanding of HistFactory systematics (normfactor, shapefactor, etc.)
JAX/PyTorch backends require separate install for GPU acceleration
ATLAS HEPData JSON format must be followed exactly
best_when: LHC/ATLAS/CMS statistical analysis with HistFactory models
CLs hypothesis testing for new physics searches
GPU-accelerated statistical fits via JAX/PyTorch backend
When ATLAS HEPData JSON statistical models are available
avoid_when: Non-HEP statistical analysis (use scipy.stats or PyMC)
When HistFactory model format is not applicable
summary: pyhf implements pure-Python HistFactory statistical models for HEP. pyhf.simplemodels.uncorrelated_background(signal, bkg, uncertainty). pyhf.infer.hypotest() for CLs. JAX/PyTorch backends for GPU. Load ATLAS HEPData JSON models. Apache-2.0 by ATLAS/scikit-HEP. Standard for LHC statistical analysis. Highly domain-specific — HEP HistFactory systematics knowledge required.
